meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional
meta.title: AWS-GAP-6: Bedrock Generative AI Design, Agents, Safety, and Operations

domains.name: Foundation Model Integration and Solution Design

questions.stem: A project team needs to make a certification-aligned design decision about selecting a foundation model for latency, cost, context window, and modality constraints. What is the BEST choice?

options.text: Use the managed capability or documented pattern for selecting a foundation model for latency, cost, context window, and modality constraints, then validate it with realistic workload or evaluation data
Choose the largest model or compute shape for every workload and ignore latency, governance, and cost constraints
Move the decision entirely to end users at runtime without documenting defaults, limits, or fallback behavior
Treat the feature as complete after a local prototype, without testing service integration or operational behavior

questions.explanation: A is correct because the supporting documentation describes selecting a foundation model for latency, cost, context window, and modality constraints as an explicit design or operational concern that should be configured and validated. B is incorrect because blindly maximizing resources ignores fit-for-purpose architecture. C is incorrect because runtime ambiguity creates inconsistent and hard-to-govern behavior. D is incorrect because a prototype alone does not prove production readiness. Reference: [Amazon Bedrock supported foundation models](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html)

questions.stem: A team is designing a solution involving using Knowledge Bases for managed retrieval-augmented generation. Which TWO actions best align with recommended practice for this objective? (Select TWO)

options.text: Apply using Knowledge Bases for managed retrieval-augmented generation deliberately and verify the behavior with representative test cases
Rely on manual, undocumented steps because managed AI and ML services infer the intended production behavior automatically
Monitor the outcome of using Knowledge Bases for managed retrieval-augmented generation with service metrics, logs, evaluations, or review evidence appropriate to the risk
Bypass access controls and validation temporarily so the workflow can collect more context from every available source

questions.explanation: A and C are correct because the referenced documentation supports intentional configuration of using Knowledge Bases for managed retrieval-augmented generation and operational verification. B is incorrect because production AI and ML systems require explicit, repeatable design rather than undocumented manual behavior. D is incorrect because broad bypass of controls conflicts with secure and governed implementation. Reference: [Amazon Bedrock supported foundation models](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html)

Reference: [Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A project team needs to make a certification-aligned design decision about tracking prompt and model versions for reproducibility. What is the BEST choice?

options.text: Use the managed capability or documented pattern for tracking prompt and model versions for reproducibility, then validate it with realistic workload or evaluation data
Choose the largest model or compute shape for every workload and ignore latency, governance, and cost constraints
Move the decision entirely to end users at runtime without documenting defaults, limits, or fallback behavior
Treat the feature as complete after a local prototype, without testing service integration or operational behavior

questions.explanation: A is correct because the supporting documentation describes tracking prompt and model versions for reproducibility as an explicit design or operational concern that should be configured and validated. B is incorrect because blindly maximizing resources ignores fit-for-purpose architecture. C is incorrect because runtime ambiguity creates inconsistent and hard-to-govern behavior. questions.stem: A team is designing a solution involving building test datasets that represent expected user tasks. Which TWO actions best align with recommended practice for this objective? (Select TWO)

options.text: Apply building test datasets that represent expected user tasks deliberately and verify the behavior with representative test cases
Rely on manual, undocumented steps because managed AI and ML services infer the intended production behavior automatically
Monitor the outcome of building test datasets that represent expected user tasks with service metrics, logs, evaluations, or review evidence appropriate to the risk
Bypass access controls and validation temporarily so the workflow can collect more context from every available source

questions.explanation: A and C are correct because the referenced documentation supports intentional configuration of building test datasets that represent expected user tasks and operational verification. B is incorrect because production AI and ML systems require explicit, repeatable design rather than undocumented manual behavior. D is incorrect because broad bypass of controls conflicts with secure and governed implementation. Reference: [Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)
